revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amduat); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1213960951 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Ancient Egypt|importance=high|religion=yes|religion-importance=high}} }} ==Fiction== References to Amduat in modern fiction: In the [[British Broadcasting Company|BBC]] television series [[Doctor Who]], episode 82, [[Pyramids of Mars]], the two principal characters discuss "the 740 gods whose names were recorded in the tomb of [[Thutmose III|Thuthmose III]]." Whether the number 740 comes from some actual source, or the fancy of the writers, is undetermined. Presumably this list of gods refers to the Amduat. I found this of some small interest and considered including it in the actual Amduat article. However, as it may be misinterpreted as contrary to the purpose of the article, I opted to place it here instead, for others to discuss and use in the future as they see fit. If others find more references to the Amduat in modern fiction, perhaps a combination of such examples could eventually find their way into the actual page. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == "that which is in the duat"? == Does amduat literally mean "that which is in the duat"? Thanks. --[[User:HilmarHansWerner|HilmarHansWerner]] ([[User talk:HilmarHansWerner|talk]]) 04:48, 10 January 2017 (UTC) 'm duat' translates as 'in the duat', so it's fair to say [that which is in] is implied, although as far as I know this term is a modern invention. : The first word is the [[nisba]] adjective [[wikt:jmj|jmj]] rather than the preposition [[wikt:m#Egyptian|m]], so the literal meaning is ‘being in the duat’; when used as a noun, it would then mean ‘(what is) being in the duat’ or, in other words, ‘that which is in the duat’. [[User:Vorziblix|Vorziblix]] ([[User talk:Vorziblix|talk]]) 03:03, 24 November 2018 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Wiki Education assignment: 311_History of Ancient Egypt== {{dashboard.wikiedu.org assignment | course = Wikipedia:Wiki_Ed/Western_Washington_University/311_History_of_Ancient_Egypt_(Winter_Quarter) | assignments = [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] | reviewers = [[User:Hippolotamus|Hippolotamus]], [[User:Hush Muninn|Hush Muninn]] | start_date = 2024-01-09 | end_date = 2024-03-22 }} — Assignment last updated by [[User:Johnstoncl|Johnstoncl]] ([[User talk:Johnstoncl|talk]]) 18:18, 2 March 2024 (UTC) I am adding my own contributions to the Amduat page, hopefully they are a good, thoughtful addition to the article! I really loved writing this article honestly, learning more about the Egyptian Underworld and how the stories about it are told is facsinating to me. Seeing the dedication and detail that was put into the walls and architecture of tombs with the Amduat was absolutely amazing. - [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] March 15, 2024 — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|undated]] comment added 04:19, 16 March 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amduat); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1213960951 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Ancient Egypt|importance=high|religion=yes|religion-importance=high}} }} ==Fiction== References to Amduat in modern fiction: In the [[British Broadcasting Company|BBC]] television series [[Doctor Who]], episode 82, [[Pyramids of Mars]], the two principal characters discuss "the 740 gods whose names were recorded in the tomb of [[Thutmose III|Thuthmose III]]." Whether the number 740 comes from some actual source, or the fancy of the writers, is undetermined. Presumably this list of gods refers to the Amduat. I found this of some small interest and considered including it in the actual Amduat article. However, as it may be misinterpreted as contrary to the purpose of the article, I opted to place it here instead, for others to discuss and use in the future as they see fit. If others find more references to the Amduat in modern fiction, perhaps a combination of such examples could eventually find their way into the actual page. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == "that which is in the duat"? == Does amduat literally mean "that which is in the duat"? Thanks. --[[User:HilmarHansWerner|HilmarHansWerner]] ([[User talk:HilmarHansWerner|talk]]) 04:48, 10 January 2017 (UTC) 'm duat' translates as 'in the duat', so it's fair to say [that which is in] is implied, although as far as I know this term is a modern invention. : The first word is the [[nisba]] adjective [[wikt:jmj|jmj]] rather than the preposition [[wikt:m#Egyptian|m]], so the literal meaning is ‘being in the duat’; when used as a noun, it would then mean ‘(what is) being in the duat’ or, in other words, ‘that which is in the duat’. [[User:Vorziblix|Vorziblix]] ([[User talk:Vorziblix|talk]]) 03:03, 24 November 2018 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Wiki Education assignment: 311_History of Ancient Egypt== {{dashboard.wikiedu.org assignment | course = Wikipedia:Wiki_Ed/Western_Washington_University/311_History_of_Ancient_Egypt_(Winter_Quarter) | assignments = [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] | reviewers = [[User:Hippolotamus|Hippolotamus]], [[User:Hush Muninn|Hush Muninn]] | start_date = 2024-01-09 | end_date = 2024-03-22 }} — Assignment last updated by [[User:Johnstoncl|Johnstoncl]] ([[User talk:Johnstoncl|talk]]) 18:18, 2 March 2024 (UTC) I am adding my own contributions to the Amduat page, hopefully they are a good, thoughtful addition to the article! I really loved writing this article honestly, learning more about the Egyptian Underworld and how the stories about it are told is facsinating to me. Seeing the dedication and detail that was put into the walls and architecture of tombs with the Amduat was absolutely amazing. - [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] March 15, 2024 — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|undated]] comment added 04:19, 16 March 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amduat); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-25, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1213960951 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Ancient Egypt|importance=high|religion=yes|religion-importance=high}} }} ==Fiction== References to Amduat in modern fiction: In the [[British Broadcasting Company|BBC]] television series [[Doctor Who]], episode 82, [[Pyramids of Mars]], the two principal characters discuss "the 740 gods whose names were recorded in the tomb of [[Thutmose III|Thuthmose III]]." Whether the number 740 comes from some actual source, or the fancy of the writers, is undetermined. Presumably this list of gods refers to the Amduat. I found this of some small interest and considered including it in the actual Amduat article. However, as it may be misinterpreted as contrary to the purpose of the article, I opted to place it here instead, for others to discuss and use in the future as they see fit. If others find more references to the Amduat in modern fiction, perhaps a combination of such examples could eventually find their way into the actual page. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == "that which is in the duat"? == Does amduat literally mean "that which is in the duat"? Thanks. --[[User:HilmarHansWerner|HilmarHansWerner]] ([[User talk:HilmarHansWerner|talk]]) 04:48, 10 January 2017 (UTC) 'm duat' translates as 'in the duat', so it's fair to say [that which is in] is implied, although as far as I know this term is a modern invention. : The first word is the [[nisba]] adjective [[wikt:jmj|jmj]] rather than the preposition [[wikt:m#Egyptian|m]], so the literal meaning is ‘being in the duat’; when used as a noun, it would then mean ‘(what is) being in the duat’ or, in other words, ‘that which is in the duat’. [[User:Vorziblix|Vorziblix]] ([[User talk:Vorziblix|talk]]) 03:03, 24 November 2018 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC) ==Wiki Education assignment: 311_History of Ancient Egypt== {{dashboard.wikiedu.org assignment | course = Wikipedia:Wiki_Ed/Western_Washington_University/311_History_of_Ancient_Egypt_(Winter_Quarter) | assignments = [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] | reviewers = [[User:Hippolotamus|Hippolotamus]], [[User:Hush Muninn|Hush Muninn]] | start_date = 2024-01-09 | end_date = 2024-03-22 }} — Assignment last updated by [[User:Johnstoncl|Johnstoncl]] ([[User talk:Johnstoncl|talk]]) 18:18, 2 March 2024 (UTC) I am adding my own contributions to the Amduat page, hopefully they are a good, thoughtful addition to the article! I really loved writing this article honestly, learning more about the Egyptian Underworld and how the stories about it are told is facsinating to me. Seeing the dedication and detail that was put into the walls and architecture of tombs with the Amduat was absolutely amazing. - [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] March 15, 2024 — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|undated]] comment added 04:19, 16 March 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: Bot: migrate talk page structure; import discussion seed (en:Amduat); add local discussion section + dated import note
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1213960951 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Ancient Egypt|importance=high|religion=yes|religion-importance=high}} }} ==Fiction== References to Amduat in modern fiction: In the [[British Broadcasting Company|BBC]] television series [[Doctor Who]], episode 82, [[Pyramids of Mars]], the two principal characters discuss "the 740 gods whose names were recorded in the tomb of [[Thutmose III|Thuthmose III]]." Whether the number 740 comes from some actual source, or the fancy of the writers, is undetermined. Presumably this list of gods refers to the Amduat. I found this of some small interest and considered including it in the actual Amduat article. However, as it may be misinterpreted as contrary to the purpose of the article, I opted to place it here instead, for others to discuss and use in the future as they see fit. If others find more references to the Amduat in modern fiction, perhaps a combination of such examples could eventually find their way into the actual page. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == "that which is in the duat"? == Does amduat literally mean "that which is in the duat"? Thanks. --[[User:HilmarHansWerner|HilmarHansWerner]] ([[User talk:HilmarHansWerner|talk]]) 04:48, 10 January 2017 (UTC) 'm duat' translates as 'in the duat', so it's fair to say [that which is in] is implied, although as far as I know this term is a modern invention. : The first word is the [[nisba]] adjective [[wikt:jmj|jmj]] rather than the preposition [[wikt:m#Egyptian|m]], so the literal meaning is ‘being in the duat’; when used as a noun, it would then mean ‘(what is) being in the duat’ or, in other words, ‘that which is in the duat’. [[User:Vorziblix|Vorziblix]] ([[User talk:Vorziblix|talk]]) 03:03, 24 November 2018 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Wiki Education assignment: 311_History of Ancient Egypt== {{dashboard.wikiedu.org assignment | course = Wikipedia:Wiki_Ed/Western_Washington_University/311_History_of_Ancient_Egypt_(Winter_Quarter) | assignments = [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] | reviewers = [[User:Hippolotamus|Hippolotamus]], [[User:Hush Muninn|Hush Muninn]] | start_date = 2024-01-09 | end_date = 2024-03-22 }} — Assignment last updated by [[User:Johnstoncl|Johnstoncl]] ([[User talk:Johnstoncl|talk]]) 18:18, 2 March 2024 (UTC) I am adding my own contributions to the Amduat page, hopefully they are a good, thoughtful addition to the article! I really loved writing this article honestly, learning more about the Egyptian Underworld and how the stories about it are told is facsinating to me. Seeing the dedication and detail that was put into the walls and architecture of tombs with the Amduat was absolutely amazing. - [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] March 15, 2024 — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|undated]] comment added 04:19, 16 March 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC)

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Amduat here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{talk page header}} == Local discussion == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:40, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 21:25, 25 February 2026 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 25 February 2026 (UTC) == Imported from English Wikipedia (2026-02-26, [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:PermanentLink/1213960951 revision]) == :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|1= {{WikiProject Ancient Egypt|importance=high|religion=yes|religion-importance=high}} }} ==Fiction== References to Amduat in modern fiction: In the [[British Broadcasting Company|BBC]] television series [[Doctor Who]], episode 82, [[Pyramids of Mars]], the two principal characters discuss "the 740 gods whose names were recorded in the tomb of [[Thutmose III|Thuthmose III]]." Whether the number 740 comes from some actual source, or the fancy of the writers, is undetermined. Presumably this list of gods refers to the Amduat. I found this of some small interest and considered including it in the actual Amduat article. However, as it may be misinterpreted as contrary to the purpose of the article, I opted to place it here instead, for others to discuss and use in the future as they see fit. If others find more references to the Amduat in modern fiction, perhaps a combination of such examples could eventually find their way into the actual page. :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) == "that which is in the duat"? == Does amduat literally mean "that which is in the duat"? Thanks. --[[User:HilmarHansWerner|HilmarHansWerner]] ([[User talk:HilmarHansWerner|talk]]) 04:48, 10 January 2017 (UTC) 'm duat' translates as 'in the duat', so it's fair to say [that which is in] is implied, although as far as I know this term is a modern invention. : The first word is the [[nisba]] adjective [[wikt:jmj|jmj]] rather than the preposition [[wikt:m#Egyptian|m]], so the literal meaning is ‘being in the duat’; when used as a noun, it would then mean ‘(what is) being in the duat’ or, in other words, ‘that which is in the duat’. [[User:Vorziblix|Vorziblix]] ([[User talk:Vorziblix|talk]]) 03:03, 24 November 2018 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC) ==Wiki Education assignment: 311_History of Ancient Egypt== {{dashboard.wikiedu.org assignment | course = Wikipedia:Wiki_Ed/Western_Washington_University/311_History_of_Ancient_Egypt_(Winter_Quarter) | assignments = [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] | reviewers = [[User:Hippolotamus|Hippolotamus]], [[User:Hush Muninn|Hush Muninn]] | start_date = 2024-01-09 | end_date = 2024-03-22 }} — Assignment last updated by [[User:Johnstoncl|Johnstoncl]] ([[User talk:Johnstoncl|talk]]) 18:18, 2 March 2024 (UTC) I am adding my own contributions to the Amduat page, hopefully they are a good, thoughtful addition to the article! I really loved writing this article honestly, learning more about the Egyptian Underworld and how the stories about it are told is facsinating to me. Seeing the dedication and detail that was put into the walls and architecture of tombs with the Amduat was absolutely amazing. - [[User:Eggiee!|Eggiee!]] March 15, 2024 — Preceding [[Wikipedia:Signatures|undated]] comment added 04:19, 16 March 2024 (UTC) :Dummy comment added by script to avoid immediate auto-archive of a fresh page.[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:42, 26 February 2026 (UTC)
